finding: Focused validators can drift into ad hoc string-anchor checks that mix source-contract verification with behavior-regression expectations, which makes it too easy to "bind the whole spec" when a smaller governance contract would be more stable and faster.
expectedBehavior: Contract validators should share a small source-anchor helper so they can verify the minimum public governance contract explicitly, while behavior/regression tests stay in their own focused spec lanes.
evidenceOrRepro: Repaired on 2026-06-28 by extracting shared source-contract anchor collection into `scripts/lib/validator-envelope.ts` and migrating `scripts/validate-branch-commit-queue.ts` to use that helper for contract anchors instead of open-coded one-off `includes(...)` chains. This keeps the branch queue validator focused on the public governance contract while leaving behavioral regression coverage in the taskflow and git-governance tests.
followUp: Reuse the shared source-contract helper for future contract validators instead of binding them to large focused specs or duplicating inline anchor scans.
